title: Multiple Polylogarithms, Algebraic K-Theory, and the Steinberg Module
speaker: Daniil Rudenko
abstract: Multiple polylogarithms appear to be central to many seemingly unrelated areas of mathematics, including the volumes of hyperbolic polytopes, scissors congruence, algebraic K-theory, and special values of zeta functions. Despite this broad network of connections, the most fundamental properties of these functions, as predicted by the Goncharov program, remain conjectural. I will discuss recent progress in the Goncharov program, which is based on the connection between multiple polylogarithms and the Steinberg module. The talks are based on joint work with Steven Charlton and Danylo Radchenko, as well as on an ongoing work with Alexander Kupers and Ismael Sierra.

proof_stages.label: Goncharov's polylogarithmic model for rational K-theory
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates explicit descriptions of K_n(F)⊗Q, defines multiple polylogarithms and their universal functional equations, and states Goncharov's expected Lie coalgebra of motivic polylogarithms whose cohomology recovers graded pieces of algebraic K-theory.

proof_stages.label: Steinberg-module coefficient package
proof_stages.summary: The Steinberg module is defined as the top homology of the Tits building, described via apartments and elementary relations, and then modified to a quotient of its tensor square by decomposable products.

proof_stages.label: Main H_1(GL, Steinberg) conjecture
proof_stages.summary: The main conjecture identifies the Goncharov/mixed-Tate Lie coalgebra with H_1(GL_n(F),St_n^L(F)), with evidence from a canonical cobracket, polylog-like elements, realization in mixed Tate structures, and the weight-two and weight-three identifications with B_2 and B_3.

proof_stages.label: Torus polylogarithms and depth reduction
proof_stages.summary: For the special field Q, the speaker explains a depth-reduction theorem and defines spaces of weight-n polylogarithms on algebraic tori generated by isogeny pushforwards and quotiented by lower-depth and logarithmic-character terms.

proof_stages.label: Steinberg description of torus polylogarithm modules
proof_stages.summary: A theorem of Charlton-Radchenko-Radchenko identifies the torus polylogarithm module with St_k(Q)⊗St_k(Q)⊗Sym^{n-k}(Q^k), sending the basic polylogarithm to an explicit pair of apartments together with the weight vector.

proof_stages.label: Reduction of H_1 to a relation kernel
proof_stages.summary: Using the Steinberg presentation, the construction of a map from H_1(GL_k(Q),St_k^L) to motivic polylogarithms is reduced to constructing a GL_k(Q)-equivariant map from the kernel I of the free-module presentation, i.e. from torus polylogarithm relations.

proof_stages.label: Iterated-integral map and independence of the auxiliary functional
proof_stages.summary: A relation is sent, after choosing a nonzero linear functional, to an iterated integral from infinity to zero with poles at the functional's values on the vectors, and the key limiting/discontinuity argument shows that this is independent of the functional and therefore yields the desired polylogarithmic functional equations.
